steps: At birth (\(x=0\)), the whale's length is 157 cm — this is the y-intercept (initial value).
The whale grows at 4.6 cm per month — this is the slope (rate of change).
A linear model with slope 4.6 and y-intercept 157 is \(y = 4.6x + 157\).
explanation:
• Choice A — '\(y = 157x\)': This makes 157 the slope (growth per month) and sets the birth length to 0, both wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — '\(y = 157x + 157\)': The slope is 157 cm/month — far too fast — and the y-intercept coincidentally equals 157, but the slope is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(y = 4.6x + 4.6\)': The slope (4.6 cm/month) is correct, but the y-intercept is 4.6 instead of 157 — this says the whale was only 4.6 cm at birth. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(y = 4.6x + 157\)': Slope = 4.6 cm/month and initial length = 157 cm. Both match the problem. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
